things.name: Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew
things.description: Thailand's most sacred site — the dazzling Grand Palace complex with the revered Emerald Buddha

things.name: Wat Pho (Temple of the Reclining Buddha)
things.description: 45-metre golden reclining Buddha and Bangkok's oldest temple — also home to Thailand's first massage school

things.name: Chatuchak Weekend Market
things.description: One of the world's largest markets with over 15,000 stalls — clothes, antiques, food and art

things.name: Chao Phraya River Boat Tour
things.description: Take the express boat past temples, traditional houses and the Grand Palace for the best Bangkok perspective

things.name: Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn)
things.description: Bangkok's most iconic riverside temple — porcelain-encrusted 82-metre tower at sunrise is unforgettable

things.name: Jim Thompson House
things.description: Six connected traditional Thai houses showcasing the American silk entrepreneur's Asian art collection

things.name: Khao San Road
things.description: Bangkok's legendary backpacker street — budget accommodation, street food, pad thai and mango sticky rice

things.name: Damnoen Saduak Floating Market
things.description: Colourful morning canal market where traders sell fresh produce from traditional longtail boats

things.name: Ayutthaya Day Trip
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage former Siamese capital with magnificent ruined temples 80 km north of Bangkok

things.name: Sky Bar at Lebua (Hangover Bar)
things.description: The world's highest alfresco bar at 820 ft — iconic from The Hangover II with panoramic city views
